name: Embalse de la Rambla de Algeciras, Museo Arqueológico Los Baños y Castillo de Alhama de Murcia — return to the Sierra de la Muela
name_original: Embalse de la Rambla de Algeciras, Museo Arqueológico Los Baños y Castillo de Alhama de Murcia — vuelta a la Sierra de la Muela
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alhama De Murcia is characterized by its diverse natural landscapes, situated at the base of the Sierra Espuña and La Muela mountain ranges. The region features pine-clad mountains, deep ravines, and the unique lunar-like formations of the Barrancos de Gebas. The Valley of the River Guadalentín adds fertile orchards and vineyards to the varied terrain. This area provides an extensive network of routes for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Alhama De Murcia The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de la Rambla de Algeciras, Museo Arqueológico Los Baños y Castillo de Alhama de Murcia — return to the Sierra de la Muela, a 12.4 miles (19.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes by a reservoir, an archaeological museum, and a castle. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Castle of Alhama de Murcia – Collado Bermejo Gravel Descent loop from Alhama de Murcia, a difficult 34.3 miles (55.2 km) path. This route includes the challenging Collado Bermejo climb and offers views of the local castle. Local touring cyclists also love the Leiva Valley Trail – Espubinc Visitor Center loop from Cerro del Cristo, a 18.7 miles (30.1 km) trail leading through the Leiva Valley, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Alhama De Murcia is defined by pine-clad mountains, dramatic ravines, and the unique badlands of Barrancos de Gebas. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult climbs. The routes in Alhama De Murcia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alhama De Murcia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Alhama De Murcia?
A: Alhama De Murcia offers an extensive network of over 50 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy paths to challenging climbs, with 7 easy, 23 moderate, and 25 difficult options.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Alhama De Murcia?
A: Yes, there are several routes ideal for beginners and families. The region features 7 easy touring cycling routes. Additionally, the 'Espuroad' series within the Sierra Espuña Regional Park offers family-friendly options, connecting key attractions over approximately 80 kilometers.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes in the region?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Alhama De Murcia offers 25 difficult routes. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10322006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Alhama de Murcia – Collado Bermejo Gravel Descent loop from Alhama de Murcia&lt;/a&gt;, which features the famous and tough Collado Bermejo climb. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10321665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leiva Valley Trail – Espubinc Visitor Center loop from Cerro del Cristo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling around Alhama De Murcia?
A: Touring cycling in Alhama De Murcia offers incredibly diverse landscapes. You'll encounter the pine-clad mountains and deep ravines of the Sierra Espuña Regional Park, the unique lunar-like formations of the Barrancos de Gebas with its turquoise reservoir, and the fertile orchards and vineyards of the Valley of the River Guadalentín.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Alhama De Murcia?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Alhama De Murcia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10322006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Alhama de Murcia – Collado Bermejo Gravel Descent loop from Alhama de Murcia&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10321665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leiva Valley Trail – Espubinc Visitor Center loop from Cerro del Cristo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017188503/embalse-de-la-rambla-de-algeciras-museo-arqueologico-los-banos-y-castillo-de-alhama-de-murcia-return-to-the-sierra-de-la-muela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Rambla de Algeciras, Museo Arqueológico Los Baños y Castillo de Alhama de Murcia — return to the Sierra de la Muela&lt;/a&gt; route takes you past a reservoir, an archaeological museum, and the historic Castle of Alhama de Murcia. You can also explore natural highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2953991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Bermejo&lt;/a&gt; mountain pass or the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5650751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho de la Arboleja&lt;/a&gt; gorge.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alhama De Murcia?
A: The region generally enjoys a mild climate, making spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather, perfect for exploring the diverse landscapes of Sierra Espuña and Barrancos de Gebas. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler mornings.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is cyclist-friendly. The village of El Berro, located within Sierra Espuña, is a popular base for cyclists and hikers and offers amenities. The Fuente del Hilo restaurant is also recognized as a popular stop for cyclists, providing a good place to refuel during your ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alhama De Murcia?
A: The touring cycling routes in Alhama De Murcia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from pine-clad mountains to unique badlands, and the extensive network of routes that cater to all ability levels.

Q: Can I expect to see any wildlife while cycling in the area?
A: Yes, especially within the Sierra Espuña Regional Park, which is known for its diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for mouflons, wild boars, and various bird species, including golden eagles, particularly in the quieter sections of the routes through the natural park.

Q: Are there any specific visitor centers or information points for cyclists?
A: The Ricardo Codorníu visitor center in Sierra Espuña Regional Park provides valuable information on routes, the park's natural environment, and local amenities, which can be very helpful for planning your touring cycling adventures.

Q: What are the road conditions like for touring cycling in Alhama De Murcia?
A: The area offers a mix of road types. Within the Sierra Espuña Regional Park, you'll find paved roads that connect various towns, often described as quiet with minimal traffic, providing an enjoyable and safe cycling experience. Some routes around Barrancos de Gebas might feature more challenging terrains due to natural wear, so it's good to check route details beforehand.
tips:
The Mirador del Embalse de la Rambla de Algeciras is an observation point that offers stunning panoramic views of the reservoir and its surroundings. From this viewing point, visitors can enjoy the natural beauty of the region, including the waters of the reservoir and the mountainous landscape that surrounds it.

It is an ideal place for nature and photography lovers, as it provides an excellent opportunity to capture the serenity and majesty of the surroundings. In addition, it is a good starting point for hiking and exploring the area.

We are in Alhama de Murcia, more specifically in the Los Baños archaeological museum, near the castle.

Alhama is a municipality located in the natural region of Bajo Guadalentín, at the foot of Sierra Espuña. As a summary, if we are in the town, it is worth visiting the castle of Alhama de Murcia, the Los Baños Archaeological Museum, the church of San Lázaro among many other tourist attractions.
